landing.title: Fast landing pages
landing.headline: One strong page to test an idea, service or campaign
landing.problem: You need to check demand or launch a campaign quickly — without weeks of work or a generic template that does not convert.
landing.solution: One focused landing page: clear offer, trust signals, contact form and analytics. Live on your domain in days.
forWho: You want to test a new service or product
You run ads and need a dedicated page
You need leads before building a full site
You have notes or a rough offer — not polished copy
notForWho: You need 10+ pages on day one
You want a complex shop with payments
You have no idea what you sell yet
landing.exampleResult: A live page on your domain with a form that sends leads to Telegram or email — ready for ads or outreach.
processSteps: You send the idea, offer notes and any references
I structure the page: headline, sections, CTA
We agree on copy and design direction
I build, connect the form and go live on your domain
You get a short handoff: how to edit and track results
clientProvides: What you sell and to whom
Logo or brand colours if you have them
Photos or examples you like
Domain access (or I help you pick one)
deliverables: Contact form with Telegram or email hook
Basic SEO title, description and analytics
Live on your domain with SSL
landing.miniCase: Alesya Takun — personal brand site with payments and booking, started as a focused offer page.

faq.q: How fast can we go live?
faq.a: Most landings are online in 3–7 days after we agree on the offer and content.
No — bullet points or voice notes are fine. I turn them into page structure and suggest improvements.

faq.q: Can we add more pages later?
faq.a: Yes. Many landings grow into full business sites when the offer works.
Yes — mobile-first layout is standard, not an extra.

faq.q: Do you write in Polish / Ukrainian / Russian?
faq.a: Yes — I build multilingual sites when you need PL, UA, RU or EN.

faq.q: What if I only have Instagram?
faq.a: That is common. We pull the offer from your posts and build a proper home for it.

business.title: Business & project websites
business.headline: Multi-page site for a business, studio or service brand
business.problem: Your business lives on social or an old site — weak trust, no booking, no multilingual reach, poor search visibility.
business.solution: A structured multi-page site with services, contact flows, payments when needed and search-ready architecture.
forWho: Studios, agencies and service businesses
You need services, about, contact and maybe a blog
You want online booking or payments
You serve clients in more than one language
notForWho: You only need one campaign page
You need a full shop with 500+ SKUs on day one
You want a custom web app with user accounts
business.exampleResult: A site your clients trust: clear services, contact or booking, found in Google in your languages.
processSteps: Brief: who you serve, what pages you need, examples you like
Site map and content plan — what goes on each page
Design direction and first draft of key pages
Build, forms, integrations, SEO base
Go live, handoff and optional iteration round
clientProvides: List of services and how you describe them
Photos, logo, brand colours
Access to domain and any existing content
Decision-maker for approvals
deliverables: Multiple languages when needed (PL / UA / RU / EN)
business.miniCase: Popular Poet — theatre and courses with trilingual content, ticketing and admin tools.

faq.q: Do you work with existing brands?
faq.a: Yes — I adapt to your visual language or help shape one if you are starting fresh.
Yes — booking and payment flows are a common part of business sites.
We can launch one language first and add locales without rebuilding the site.
Depends on your needs. I recommend after we review content volume and integrations.
I set up a workflow you can use — CMS, simple admin or documented process.
I launch on modern hosting (e.g. Vercel) and show you how it works.

ecommerce.title: E-commerce setup & migration
ecommerce.headline: Shop, migration and daily ops wired for sales
ecommerce.problem: Catalog chaos, manual updates, marketplace and website out of sync — migration feels risky and expensive.
ecommerce.solution: Shopify or WooCommerce setup or migration, payments, shipping, catalog sync and content workflows that scale.
forWho: You sell physical or digital products online
You migrate from WooCommerce, WordPress or another platform
You sync with marketplaces or warehouse tools
You need multilingual product content
notForWho: You only need a brochure site
You want a marketplace with thousands of sellers
You have no products or pricing yet
ecommerce.exampleResult: A live shop with working checkout, shipping rules and catalog sync — less manual work every week.
processSteps: Audit current catalog, integrations and pain points
Pick platform and migration plan with a safe cutover date
Build store structure, payments and shipping
Migrate or sync products and test orders
Go live, monitor and fix edge cases
clientProvides: Product data export or access to current shop
Payment and shipping provider accounts
Brand assets and category structure
List of integrations you rely on
deliverables: Marketplace or warehouse sync when needed
ecommerce.miniCase: KAYER.PL — WooCommerce to Shopify migration with catalog sync and multilingual content pipeline.

faq.a: Depends on your ops — I recommend after reviewing catalog size and integrations.
We plan a cutover window and test sync before switching traffic.

faq.q: Do you connect BaseLinker or Allegro?
faq.a: Yes — catalog sync and marketplace ops are core to my e-commerce work.
I train you or your team and document the workflow.

faq.q: What about B2B or salon-only products?
faq.a: Gated access, verification flows and custom pricing — done on KAYER and similar projects.
Yes — AI-assisted content pipelines for PL / UA / EN when it saves real time.

faq.q: Do you fix slow old shops?
faq.a: Often migration plus cleanup is faster than patching a broken theme.

automation.title: Automation, bots & internal tools
automation.headline: Scripts and workflows that remove manual work
automation.problem: Content, leads and reports are done by hand — slow, error-prone, does not scale.
automation.solution: Telegram bots, publishing pipelines, API integrations, admin panels and AI-assisted workflows your team can run.
forWho: Weekly repetitive tasks (posts, reports, updates)
Lead capture or ops alerts via Telegram
Connecting shop, sheets, email or CRM
Small internal tools instead of enterprise software
notForWho: You need a full ERP replacement
The process changes every day with no rules
You want automation before the manual process works once
automation.exampleResult: A bot or script that runs the weekly job in minutes — with logs and a simple way to fix mistakes.
processSteps: You describe the manual workflow step by step
I map inputs, outputs and failure cases
Build MVP automation and test on real data
Connect to your site, shop or messenger
Document and hand off — optional monitoring
clientProvides: Walkthrough of the current manual process
Sample inputs (files, messages, exports)
Access to APIs or admin panels involved
Who should get alerts or results
deliverables: Telegram bots for leads or ops
AI parsing when it saves real time
automation.miniCase: Popular Poet Event Bot — photo and text in, structured event pages out, ~4 shows per week automated.

faq.q: What is a typical automation project?
faq.a: Weekly event publishing, lead bots, catalog updates or internal reporting dashboards.

faq.q: Do I need a developer on my side?
faq.a: No — I deliver working scripts, launch them and write clear instructions.

faq.q: Can automation connect to my existing site?
faq.a: Yes — most workflows plug into Shopify, WordPress, Next.js or warehouse tools.

faq.q: Is this the same as a chatbot for customers?
faq.a: Often yes for Telegram — but I focus on ops and sales workflows, not generic FAQ bots.
I prefer free or low-cost tiers when possible and show you expected usage.
Yes — support retainers are available after the first build.

audit.title: Website rescue & audit
audit.headline: Quick audit for old sites — UX, SEO, speed, AI visibility
audit.problem: Site looks outdated, converts poorly, does not show up in Google or AI answers — a full rebuild feels too expensive right now.
audit.solution: Structured audit with prioritized fixes, impact notes and an optional rescue plan before a full rebuild.
forWho: Old WordPress, Shopify or custom sites
Low conversions but traffic exists
Before a redesign or migration
You got outreach about SEO and want a second opinion
notForWho: You need a new site built this week
audit.exampleResult: A written report: what to fix first, what it likely improves and what a rebuild would cost.
processSteps: You send the URL and main business goal
I review UX, technical SEO, speed and AI visibility
I score issues by impact and effort
You get a prioritized action list
Optional call to plan fixes or a rebuild
clientProvides: Website URL and target market
What is not working (leads, speed, search)
Access to Search Console if you have it
Any redesign plans or constraints
deliverables: Impact notes and suggested next steps
audit.miniCase: Lead Hunting pipeline — automated audits for outreach; same checklist used for client rescues.

faq.a: A written audit with UX, SEO, speed and AI visibility findings plus a prioritized action list.

faq.q: Is this only for old WordPress sites?
faq.a: Any stack — WordPress, Shopify, custom Next.js, landing pages.

faq.q: Can you fix issues after the audit?
faq.a: Yes — audit often leads to a landing rescue or full rebuild quote.
Usually 2–5 business days depending on site size.
No — I show what is broken and what to fix first. Honest metrics only.

faq.q: Will AI tools like ChatGPT find my site?
faq.a: I check llms.txt, schema and content structure — and tell you what is missing.

creative.title: Creative & event projects
creative.headline: Site and automation for courses, studios and events
creative.problem: Project lives on Instagram — no domain, no tickets, no search visibility, manual weekly publishing.
creative.solution: Showcase plus sales plus bot for publishing. Niche proof of flexibility — see Popular Poet.
forWho: Theatres, schools, courses and event brands
Weekly schedule that changes often
Ticket or registration sales online
Multilingual audiences (PL / UA / RU)
notForWho: Large festival with complex seating maps on day one
creative.exampleResult: Your own domain, ticket or registration flow and less time spent copying posts every week.
processSteps: Map offer: events, courses, tickets, languages
Design showcase and checkout or registration
Build schedule and content structure
Add bot or automation for weekly updates if needed
Launch and train your team
clientProvides: Event or course calendar
Brand visuals and tone of voice
Ticket pricing and payment setup
Who publishes updates each week
deliverables: Telegram bot for publishing when useful
Admin and check-in tools when needed
creative.miniCase: Popular Poet / PopularTickets — trilingual theatre site, own ticketing and event automation.

faq.a: Core offers are landings, e-commerce and automation — events are a proven niche.

faq.q: Can you automate weekly event posts?
faq.a: Yes — photo plus text to structured pages to search ping is a pattern I ship.
Yes — own checkout or integration depending on your volume and fees.

faq.q: Can actors or staff update the site?
faq.a: I set up flows that match how your team actually works — often Telegram-first.
PL, UA, RU and EN are common in my event work.
